business.headline: Microsoft to cut 4,800 jobs as AI spending continues to riseThe latest round of layoffs comes at a time when major technology companies are spending billions of dollars on AI infrastructure and facing growing pressure to show returns on those investments.

EPF scheme 2026: Can employers refuse to match higher PF contributions?The new EPF Scheme 2026 has clarified the rules on voluntary PF contributions, but what does that mean for salaried employees? Can your employer say no to matching the extra contribution? Have a look.

Vietnam and 5 others climb World Bank's income ladder. Where does India stand?This year, no country moved to a lower category. Instead, six countries improved their position. Jordan, Micronesia, the Philippines, Sri Lanka and Vietnam moved from the lower-middle-income group to the upper-middle-income category.

Sensex ends 521 points higher, Nifty above 24,400; HDFC up 3%The BSE Sensex rose 521.16 points, or 0.67%, to close at 78,285.07, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 159.50 points, or 0.66%, to settle at 24,430.35.

Sebi plans to make it easier to short stocks. Here's what it means for investorsSebi is considering wider access to stock borrowing and lower collateral for short selling. The move is aimed at shifting activity towards cash equities as derivative losses mount for retail investors.

HDFC, RBL, ICICI jump up to 4%: Why are banking stocks rising today?HDFC Bank led a broad rally in banking stocks after reporting strong provisional June quarter business growth. The move lifted expectations for a solid earnings season even as analysts tracked loan mix, CASA pressure and improving macro signals.

How India kept fuel prices in check while the world burned through its walletsWhen the Strait of Hormuz shut down and crude surged past $120 a barrel, petrol prices rose 64% in Pakistan, 21% in Britain and 85% for diesel in the UAE. In Delhi, petrol went up 7.8% Here is how India managed that, and what it cost.
Apple restarts card payments for app store in India: ReportApple had stopped accepting credit and debit card payments in India in 2021 after the RBI introduced its card tokenisation mandate.

You may not be able to sell your electrical products from Oct 1. Here's whyIndia will require BIS certification and the ISI mark for 90 categories of electrical appliances from 1 October 2026. The phased rollout could disrupt manufacturing, imports and sales for businesses that miss approval timelines.

Can't activate your UAN on the EPFO portal? Here's why and what to do nowEPFO has ended UAN activation and direct UAN generation on its portal after the upgrade. The shift to UMANG with Aadhaar-based face authentication changes how PF subscribers access these services.

Made over Rs 1.25 lakh in equity gains? Here's why ITR-1 may not be enough this yearBefore filing your return, it is worth checking your capital gains statement carefully to ensure you choose the correct ITR form and avoid unnecessary notices or delays.

8th Pay Commission: Odisha talks end today. What has happened so far?The Bhubaneswar meetings, held on July 6 and 7, are part of the Commission's nationwide consultation exercise, where it is meeting employee unions, pensioner associations and other stakeholders before preparing its recommendations for the Centre.
Sensex opens 250 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; HDFC Bank up 2%The BSE Sensex was up 0.33% at 78,020.50, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 0.33% to trade at 24,349.95 around 9:30 am.

Sitting on idle gold? Government may soon give you a reason to earn from itThe Centre may soon unveil a revamped Gold Monetisation Scheme that allows jewellers to collect household gold deposits. The proposed shift is aimed at drawing idle gold into the formal system and reducing reliance on imports.

Want to become a crorepati through SIP? Here's how much to invest and for how longBecoming a crorepati is no longer just a dream for the wealthy. With the right SIP strategy and enough time, you could get there too, but how much do you really need to invest each month?

The company powering Nvidia's AI chips wants a $28 billion Wall Street debutSK Hynix is set to launch a $28 billion US listing after a sharp rally driven by AI chip demand. The offering underlines how companies supplying the hardware behind artificial intelligence are drawing huge investor interest.

Maersk places fresh order for 1,000 India-made shipping containersThe announcement came as Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Sarbananda Sonowal, unveiled the first export-import (EXIM) shipping container manufactured in India for the company at the Maersk-CONCOR Inland Container Depot in UP's Dadri.

E20 even powers F1 cars: Auto experts dismiss concerns, back govt amid backlashIndustry experts defended the rollout of E20 fuel, saying extensive testing found no concerns in compatible vehicles. They said the ethanol push is tied to lower emissions, defined standards and reduced oil dependence.

Carlsberg eyes India stock market listing; confidential IPO filing submittedCarlsberg said it has confidentially filed for an IPO of its Indian business. The proposed listing would allow a partial stake sale, subject to approvals and market conditions.

ITR 2026: Do you need to file two income tax returns? Tax department explainsThe new Income-tax Act has changed the way taxpayers talk about return filing, replacing "Assessment Year" with "Tax Year". But does that mean you'll have to file two ITRs? Here's what the tax department says.

entertainment.headline: Anurag Kashyap reviews Mollywood Times, praises director for truth and idealism Anurag Kashyap has praised Mollywood Times on Letterboxd, calling it "a lot of truth and a lot of idealism" while lauding director Abhinav Sunder Nayak's vision.

Jaswant Singh Khalra abducted again: Kunal Kamra's blistering post on Satluj ban Standup comedian Kunal Kamra has questioned CBFC chairperson Prasoon Joshi after Satluj was removed from OTT within 48 hours of its uncut release. His note has reignited the censorship debate around the film formerly titled Punjab '95.

This one Satluj scene has sparked a fierce online backlash A scene from Satluj has triggered sharp reactions online. The row has renewed scrutiny of the film's portrayal, even as its removal from Zee5 remains under focus.

Satluj: Real story of Jaswant Singh Khalra, a 16-year court battle and the murder The Satluj OTT row has renewed attention on the Jaswant Singh Khalra case, which began with his 1995 abduction after he exposed alleged secret cremations. The case led to Supreme Court intervention, a CBI probe and life terms for six Punjab Police officials.

Lock Upp: Kashmera Shah supports 'mami' Ssunita Ahuja with a poweful note Actor Kashmera Shah came out in support of Govinda's wife, influencer Ssunita Ahuja, after she broke down on Lock Upp and said she wanted to leave the show. The actor shared an emotional note urging her to be strong and reminding her that she is not alone.

Satluj X review: Diljit's film tears heart apart; stunned viewers call it hidden gem Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj has left viewers emotional, with many calling the film a "hidden gem" on X. Despite its removal from ZEE5, the film continues to receive praise for its storytelling and performances.

Infuriating: Ranvir Shorey, Sanjay Gupta and others slam Satluj's removal from OTT Actor Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj was taken off from streaming in India days after its delayed release, prompting reactions from actor Ranvir Shorey, directors Sanjay Gupta and Onir. The move has renewed attention on the film's politically sensitive subject and troubled certification journey.

Sikh body slams removal of Satluj, calls it attempt to suppress historical truth Shiromani Gurdwara Parbandhak Committee president Harjinder Singh Dhami condemned the removal of Satluj from ZEE5 in India. He called it an attempt to suppress historical truth and demanded that the restrictions be lifted immediately.

No pro-Khalistan sentiment, shows truth: Khalra's lawyer on Satluj's OTT removal The takedown of Satluj after its brief Zee5 release has drawn sharply different reactions from people linked to the Jaswant Khalra case. While Advocate Navkiran Singh called the move wrong, former IAS officer KBS Sidhu said it may have been done to safeguard public order.

Aditya Dhar, Arjun Rampal praise force of nature Ranveer Singh on 41st birthday Director Aditya Dhar and actor Arjun Rampal marked actor Ranveer Singh's 41st birthday with unseen Dhurandhar pictures and personal notes. Their messages focused on his performance in the film and their shared bond forged on set.

ZEE5 breaks silence on Satluj takedown, urges viewers not to turn to piracy ZEE5 has issued a fresh statement after pulling down Satluj, urging viewers not to support piracy and assuring them it is exploring every possible way to restore the Diljit Dosanjh-starrer. The film, based on the life of human rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra, had faced years of censorship hurdles before its surprise OTT release.

Arjun Kapoor is happier than anyone else after sister Anshula's kaleera falls on him Actor Arjun Kapoor appeared happier than anyone else after sister Anshula Kapoor's kaleera fell on him during her chooda ceremony. Sharing the video, Anshula joked that she had never seen him so "committed" to getting chosen by a kaleera.

NTK leader seeks Tamil Nadu ban on Jr NTR-Trivikram film over Murugan row Seeman has asked for a ban on Jr NTR and Trivikram Srinivas's upcoming film in Tamil Nadu over its apparent portrayal of Lord Murugan. The demand follows backlash to the announcement line 'Born in the North' and has widened the debate over faith, identity and creative liberty.

Huma Qureshi dodges question on Toxic delay, says focus should be on August release Huma Qureshi brushed aside questions on the repeated postponements of Yash's Toxic and pointed to its new August 26 release date.

Diljit Dosanjh reacts as fan asks why Satluj chose OTT over theatres Actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh has revealed why Satluj skipped a theatrical release and premiered directly on ZEE5, saying the team faced years of hurdles and was not even allowed to talk about the film before its release. The actor also reflected on the delays and thanked fans for supporting the project.

Satluj leaked online: Diljit Dosanjh's film available in HD a day after OTT removal Diljit Dosanjh's film Satluj is being widely shared online after its removal from Zee5. The circulation has intensified debate around censorship, free speech and the reach of digital distribution.

Satluj review: When a democracy forgets its dead, cinema remembersRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026 Satluj movie review: Diljit Dosanjh's film follows Jaswant Singh Khalra as he traces the disappeared through official records and cremation logs. The film turns that investigation into a stark question about memory, accountability and constitutional rights.

Pradeep Ranganathan turns producer, joins forces with Mamitha, Ashwath Marimuthu Actor-director Pradeep Ranganathan launched his home banner PR Show with PRS01, a multilingual film starring Mamitha Baiju and director Ashwath Marimuthu, while also marking his full-fledged debut as a producer.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan: Fake censor certificate sparks curiosity amid release buzz A fake CBFC certificate for CM Vijay's Jana Nayagan circulated online, claiming the film had received an A rating. However, it was soon pointed out that the censor certificate for Leo had been edited to make it appear as Jana Nayagan's.

general news.headline: Champat Rai resigns from Ram temple trust, Krishna Mohan is new interim chiefThe Ram Mandir Trust has accepted the resignations of Champat Rai and Anil Mishra after a marathon meeting on the donation embezzlement case. The Trust also broke its silence over the controversy.

2 Assam Rifles soldiers killed in ambush by militants in Manipur's UkhrulManipur: The attack took place at around 1.30 pm in the Nungshang Khong area when militants opened fire on a convoy of the 40 Assam Rifles.

E10 should continue or entire vehicle fleet…: Old Niti Aayog report flags concernThe six-year-old roadmap shows that while carmakers' body SIAM backed India's ethanol-blending programme, it also pushed for parallel E10 availability, arguing that existing vehicles would need protection-grade fuel during the transition to higher ethanol blends.

Sooryavanshi in, Samson out. Rinku back as BCCI announces squad for Zimbabwe T20IsIndia tour of Zimbabwe: Shreyas Iyer will lead a new-look squad for the three-match T20I series in Zimbabwe, starting July 23. While Rinku Singh is back, India have left out Sanju Samson.

Bengal rape-murder: How CCTV footage of girl's final hours led locals to suspectA CCTV clue led residents to a suspect after a 12-year-old girl went missing in Baruipur, near Kolkata. The suspect's alleged disclosure led to the recovery of her body from a pond, sparking widespread protests, a mob lynching and a political face-off.

At Tehran's Azadi Square, Khamenei's ringed hand is new symbol of defianceMillions gathered in Tehran as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's coffin moved from Grand Mosalla to Azadi Square. A giant ringed-hand monument and delegations from nearly 100 countries underscored the funeral's symbolic and diplomatic weight.

Among the protesters were ardent BJP supporters and a person who said he had been linked to the RSS for decades.

At Khamenei's funeral, did Iran use Quran verses to convey messages to countries?Different Quranic verses were recited as delegations from various countries and non-state entities paid their last respects to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran. Experts suggest the recitations of the verses at the funeral appeared to be messages.

Maharashtra bans Pak-made Goree Beauty Cream over toxic mercury, no expiry dateOfficials in Maharashtra have banned Goree Beauty Cream, a Pakistan-made skin-lightening cream, after tests found toxic mercury and no expiry date on the product. The action followed complaints from Nagpur, where users developed kidney-related ailments.

Bhutan journalist doubles down after India debunks report on E20 petrol offerThe exchange has evolved into a dispute over the record itself, with The Bhutanese newspaper citing official Bhutanese correspondence and India's Petroleum Ministry maintaining that no OMC ever offered E20 petrol.

Indian man posed as CIA agent, wooed Indonesian President for defence dealsUS-based businessman Gaurav Srivastava, who was born in Lucknow, allegedly posed as a CIA agent to gain access to Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto and his inner circle, and secure preliminary defence agreements. Investigations said the proposed deals never materialised, while Srivastava denied posing as a CIA operative and rejected all allegations.

Satluj: Real story of Jaswant Singh Khalra, a 16-year court battle and the murderThe Satluj OTT row has renewed attention on the Jaswant Singh Khalra case, which began with his 1995 abduction after he exposed alleged secret cremations. The case led to Supreme Court intervention, a CBI probe and life terms for six Punjab Police officials.

Live: 17 flights cancelled, 365 delayed as downpour disrupts MumbaiMonsoon Tracker Live Updates: Heavy monsoon showers swept across several parts of the country on Sunday (July 5, 2026), with Mumbai witnessing severe disruption, while weather authorities issued alerts for Kerala, Odisha and Jharkhand. Delhi also experienced widespread rainfall as monsoon activity gathered pace. Mumbai bore the brunt of the spell after intense overnight rain led to the suspension of runway operations at the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport for about an hour. Official figures showed that several parts of the city received more than 200 mm of rainfall over the past 24 hours, while a few locations recorded nearly 300 mm. In eastern India, a well-marked low-pressure area over the Bay of Bengal strengthened into a depression, prompting Odisha to place all its districts on high alert. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the weather system is expected to cross the north Odisha coast between Chandbali and Digha within the next day, bringing heavy rainfall to Odisha and neighbouring West Bengal. Kerala also remained under the influence of active monsoon conditions, with widespread showers reported across most parts of the state. The IMD issued orange and yellow alerts for multiple districts. Meanwhile, Jharkhand has been placed under a yellow alert for thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds across all 24 districts from July 6 to July 9. Stay tuned with indiatoday.in for all live updates.

How Vijay is redefining Opposition politics: Target BJP, but tango with CentreFor C Joseph Vijay, opposing the BJP seemingly does not mean opposing the Centre. Even as he has positioned the BJP as his principal ideological enemy, the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister is pursuing a pragmatic working relationship with the Modi government, separating political battles from administrative priorities.

Row over retired Army officer's 'Many more to come' post on Aamir Khan's marriageA retired Lieutenant General's comment on Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt's wedding drew immediate scrutiny online. The exchange widened into a debate on X.

Former UK minister says ex-colonies owe Britain for empire's development workFormer UK Home Secretary Suella Braverman, whose parents are of Indian origin, has stirred controversy by saying that countries once colonised by Britain should repay the United Kingdom for the investments made during the British Empire. Her remarks came as Jamaica prepares to petition King Charles III for reparations over Britain's role in slavery.

Man finds rain-soaked diary on roadside, every page tells a heartwarming storyA man found a rain-soaked diary found on a roadside and appealed for help tracing its owner. The handwritten keepsake stirred viewers, who hoped the damaged memories could still be recovered.

Watch: Harry Kane gets his voice back after viral World Cup interview Harry Kane laughed off the viral reaction after regaining his voice following England's win over Mexico. The squeaky interview became a hit, capping chaotic celebrations in Mexico City on Sunday evening.

India's Praggnanandhaa finishes joint third as Alireza wins Croatia Rapid & Blitz title Grand Chess Tour: Praggnanandhaa closed with a mixed blitz day in Croatia, beating Alireza Firouzja but finishing joint third. The result kept him in the Grand Chess Tour race, though St Louis leg now carries real weight.

Delhi HC sets two-week deadline for WFI to rule on Vinesh Phogat show-cause notice The Delhi High Court has ordered the WFI to decide Vinesh Phogat's show-cause notice within two weeks after giving her a personal hearing. The case keeps alive a larger dispute over exclusionary selection rules, maternity return, and governance in Indian wrestling.

World Cup: Ex-FIFA chief slams Trump-linked red card reversal for US striker Former FIFA president Sepp Blatter has slammed FIFA after the governing body controversially suspended Folarin Balogun's automatic one-match ban, allowing the United States striker to face Belgium in Monday's World Cup Round of 16 clash.

Tearful Casemiro comforted by journalists after Brazil's painful World Cup exit Casemiro struggled to hold back tears as he reflected on Brazil's heartbreaking exit from the FIFA World Cup after a stunning 2-1 defeat to the Norwegian national football team in the Round of 16 on Sunday.

Mexico boss Aguirre steps down after World Cup exit, backs Marquez as successorJavier Aguirre's third spell as Mexico coach ended on Sunday after the co-hosts suffered a 3-2 defeat to England in the World Cup Round of 16, with the veteran manager backing assistant Rafa Marquez to succeed him.

My wife is Argentine: Cristiano Ronaldo reveals funny chat with flight attendantCristiano Ronaldo recalled a funny exchange with an Argentine flight attendant before Portugal's Spain clash. The anecdote briefly eased a press conference dominated by questions over age, form and retirement.

Freak celebration injury! Jordan Henderson rushed to hospital after England winFIFA World Cup: England midfielder Jordan Henderson was rushed to hospital after suffering a serious wrist injury during the post-match celebrations following his side's dramatic 3-2 victory over Mexico in the Round of 16 on Sunday.

Dear Neymar, thank you for teaching a generation what Joga Bonito looked likeFor millions who grew up in yellow and green, Neymar was never just Brazil's greatest player of a generation. A red card, 3 phone calls from Trump and a shocking FIFA U-turnFIFA found itself at the centre of a World Cup storm on Sunday after suspending Folarin Balogun's automatic red-card ban, allowing the United States striker to play against Belgium. The decision followed reported intervention from US President Donald Trump, who personally urged FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review the case.

I've lost my voice: Harry Kane revels after England oust Mexico in Azteca epicHarry Kane could barely speak after England's thrilling 3-2 win over Mexico in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16, admitting he had "lost his voice" after celebrating a famous victory with teammates and travelling supporters at the Azteca Stadium.

Watch: Ochoa retires in tears as England end Mexico's World Cup dreamGuillermo Ochoa's storied career ended in heartbreak on Sunday as the legendary goalkeeper retired after Mexico's 3-2 defeat to England in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16.

England survive the fires of Azteca to end Mexico's World Cup dreamEngland survived a dramatic Azteca thriller to beat co-hosts Mexico 3-2 and book their place in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals. Why Bruno Guimaraes took Brazil's penalty over Vini Jr vs Norway? Ancelotti explainsCarlo Ancelotti revealed Bruno Guimaraes was Brazil's third-choice penalty taker against Norway after Neymar and Raphinha were unavailable. Neymar retires from international football after Brazil's World Cup exitFIFA World Cup: Neymar has retired from international football after Brazil's shock Round of 16 exit to Norway at the World Cup. MEX 2-3 ENG Highlights: England knock Mexico out of World CupMexico (Mexico) vs England (ENG) Highlights, FIFA World Cup Round of 16: England have booked their place in the World Cup quarter-finals with a hard-fought 3-2 victory over Mexico. Erling Haaland ends Brazil's sleepy dream of a sixth World Cup with just 2 shotsCarlo Ancelotti built a Brazil that refused to lose control. Against Norway, they controlled so little of the game that Erling Haaland only needed two chances to end their dreams of a sixth FIFA World Cup title.

Jannik Sinner scripts another slice of history, sweeps into Wimbledon quartersDefending champion Jannik Sinner stormed into his fifth consecutive Wimbledon quarterfinal with a dominant straight-sets win over Shintaro Mochizuki, extending his winning streak at the All England Club to 11.

Cristiano Ronaldo confirms he's playing his last World CupCristiano Ronaldo confirmed the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be the last of his illustrious career, saying he wants to savour every moment of the tournament. Sabalenka vents frustration after Wimbledon exit: Want to get completely drunkAryna Sabalenka admitted she wanted to get completely drunk after her Wimbledon title hopes ended with a straight-sets defeat to Naomi Osaka, who outplayed the World No. 1 to reach her maiden quarter-final at the All England Club.
